courses.name: CS 370: Human Computer Interaction
courses.description: Design of effective human-computer interfaces using a human-centered approach. (Includes Codex Content)

184.title: Interaction Design Fundamentals
184.first_principles: ["Mentor: What constitutes the boundary between human intent and machine execution?","Student: The interface, or rather, the syntactic layer of interaction.","Mentor: Precisely. Axiom 1: Visibility. If the state is opaque, the user is blind. The system must project its internal topology onto the sensory surface.","Student: But too much visibility leads to cognitive noise.","Mentor: Axiom 2: Constraints. We must bound the agent's agency to prevent state-space collapse. Direct the user's flow through logical and physical barriers.","Axiom 3: Feedback. Every action must have an equal and perceptible reaction. The cybernetic loop must be closed within milliseconds."]
184.architectural_logic:
graph TD
    subgraph Human [The Human Cognitive Processor]
        A[Goal] --> B[Intention]
        B --> C[Action Sequence]
    end
    subgraph Machine [The Machine Syntactic Processor]
        E[Display] --> F[Interpretation]
        F --> G[Evaluation]
    end
    C -->|Interface| E
    G -->|Feedback| A
    style Human fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1
    style Machine fill:#0f172a,stroke:#4f46e5

184.title: User Research & Framing
184.first_principles: ["Axiom 1: Radical Empathy. Transcending personal projection to perceive the user's authentic phenomenological experience.","Axiom 2: Framing. The cognitive scaffolding that dictates how information is processed and interpreted.","Student: Is research the search for truth or the search for utility?","Mentor: In HCI, utility IS the truth. Axiom 3: Personas as Behavioral Models. Escaping demographic caricatures to focus on goal-oriented archetypes."]
184.architectural_logic:
mindmap
  root((User Research Architecture))
    Qualitative Epistemology
      Phenomenological Interviews
      Ethnographic Observation
      Contextual Inquiry
    Quantitative Analysis
      Statistical Significance
      A-B Multivariate Testing
      Eye-Tracking Heatmaps

184.title: Ideation & Sketching
184.first_principles: ["Axiom 1: Ideational Variance. Maximizing the entropy of the design space before selecting for convergence.","Axiom 2: The Sketch as Cognitive Tool. Externalizing thoughts through low-fidelity representations to facilitate reflective dialogue.","Mentor: Why sketch when you can prototype?","Student: To minimize the cost of being wrong."]
184.architectural_logic:
graph LR
    A[Problem Space] --> B((Divergence))
    B --> C(Brainstorming)
    C --> D(Sketching)
    D --> E((Convergence))
    E --> F{Selection}
    F --> G[Design Solution]

184.title: Prototyping & Evaluation
184.first_principles: ["Axiom 1: Fidelity-Goal Isomorphism. Selecting the minimum viable representation to answer specific research hypotheses.","Axiom 2: Heuristic Rigor. Systematic evaluation against formal usability principles to identify latent design flaws.","Student: When is a prototype 'finished'?","Mentor: When it reveals the truth about the user's struggle."]
184.architectural_logic:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant P as Prototype
    participant E as Evaluator
    U->>P: Action (Mental Model Application)
    P-->>U: State Feedback
    U-->>E: Verbal Protocol (Thinking Aloud)
    E->>E: Note Heuristic Violation
